title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: Load libraries use analysis.
code: # remotes::install_github("data-mermaid/mermaidr") # remotes::install_github("data-mermaid/mermaidr-covariates")  library(mermaidr) library(mermaidrcovariates) library(tidyverse)

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: first step get coral cover data GFCR projects MERMAID. following gets summary sample events, filters projects whose tags contain “GFCR”, projects hard coral cover data.
code: summary_sampleevents <- mermaid_get_summary_sampleevents()  gfcr_summary_sampleevents <- summary_sampleevents %>%   filter(str_detect(tags, "GFCR")) %>%   filter(!is.na(`benthicpit_percent_cover_benthic_category_avg_Hard coral`)) %>%   rename(hard_coral_cover = `benthicpit_percent_cover_benthic_category_avg_Hard coral`)  gfcr_summary_sampleevents ## # A tibble: 142 × 385 ##    project_id project tags  country site_id site  latitude longitude reef_type reef_zone ##    <chr>      <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>     <chr>     ##  1 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  2 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  3 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  4 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  5 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  6 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  7 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  8 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ##  9 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef ## 10 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  04e931… Nort…     29.5      35.0 fringing  fore reef ## # ℹ 132 more rows ## # ℹ 375 more variables: reef_exposure <chr>, management_id <chr>, management <chr>, ## #   management_est_year <int>, management_size <dbl>, management_parties <chr>, ## #   management_compliance <chr>, management_rules <chr>, sample_date <date>, ## #   data_policy_beltfish <chr>, data_policy_benthiclit <chr>, ## #   data_policy_benthicpit <chr>, data_policy_benthicpqt <chr>, ## #   data_policy_habitatcomplexity <chr>, data_policy_bleachingqc <chr>, …

what: Summary of hard coral cover for projects
title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: Summarise projects show tags, country, number sites, average hard coral cover.
code: gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%   group_by(project, tags, country) %>%   summarise(     n_sites = length(site),     average_hard_coral_cover = mean(hard_coral_cover), .groups = "drop"   ) ## # A tibble: 7 × 5 ##   project                                   tags  country n_sites average_hard_coral_c…¹ ##   <chr>                                     <chr> <chr>     <int>                  <dbl> ## 1 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme   Glob… Jordan       29                  21.4  ## 2 Investing in Coral Reefs and the Blue Ec… Unit… Fiji         53                  30.9  ## 3 Maldives RREEF (GFCR)                     Glob… Maldiv…      11                  43.4  ## 4 SLCRI Bar Reef Seascape                   Inte… Sri La…      11                   9.68 ## 5 SLCRI Kayankerni Seascape                 Inte… Sri La…      11                  44.4  ## 6 SLCRI Pigeon Island Seascape              Inte… Sri La…       7                  55.9  ## 7 Terumbu Karang Sehat Indonesia Program -… Cons… Indone…      20                  36.9  ## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​average_hard_coral_cover

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: Next, get relevant covariates sites.

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: List available covariates.
code: list_covariates() ## # A tibble: 17 × 11 ##    title          description start_date end_date   license `sci:doi` keywords providers ##    <chr>          <chr>       <date>     <date>     <chr>   <chr>     <chr>    <list>    ##  1 50 Reefs+ pri… This datas… 2026-02-22 2026-02-22 CC-BY-… 10.5281/… conserv… <tibble>  ##  2 ACA Benthic H… The Alan C… 2018-01-01 2021-01-01 propri… <NA>      ACA, Al… <tibble>  ##  3 ACA Reef Exte… ACA reef e… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      reef, r… <tibble>  ##  4 Country Bound… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble>  ##  5 Daily Sea Sur… Sea surfac… 1985-01-01 2026-01-13 CC0-1.0 <NA>      climate… <tibble>  ##  6 Human populat… Coastal po… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coastal… <tibble>  ##  7 Marine Ecoreg… A biogeogr… 2011-11-18 2011-11-18 CC-BY-… 10.1641/… biodive… <tibble>  ##  8 Market gravit… From econo… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  ##  9 Number of por… Port locat… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  ## 10 Tourism index  Intensive … 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  ## 11 GPW Country B… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble>  ## 12 GPW Dispersal… Dispersal … 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      dispers… <tibble>  ## 13 GPW Global Se… Global Sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 propri… <NA>      exposur… <tibble>  ## 14 GPW Global Se… Global sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, se… <tibble>  ## 15 GPW Land Use … Land Use a… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0-1.0 <NA>      land co… <tibble>  ## 16 GPW MEOW Real… Marine Eco… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 cc-by-… <NA>      biodive… <tibble>  ## 17 GPW Watersheds Watersheds… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, hy… <tibble>  ## # ℹ 3 more variables: `sci:citation` <chr>, bbox <list>, id <chr>

what: Get maximum SST for previous year
title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: Focus Sea Surface Temperature, get max 30 days prior survey data. Review survey data: can access daily SST data using function get_zonal_statistics(), takes site latitude longitude, well survey date, find data site n_days days prior, within given radius, spatially aggregates according specified spatial_stat. example, get daily SST 30 days prior (including) sample event, using mean SST within 100m sites: Look returned data, keeping project information, site, survey date, hard coral cover, zonal_statistics
code: gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%   distinct(project, site, latitude, longitude, sample_date) ## # A tibble: 142 × 5 ##    project                                 site           latitude longitude sample_date ##    <chr>                                   <chr>             <dbl>     <dbl> <date>      ##  1 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-06  ##  2 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-01  ##  3 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-10-28  ##  4 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-09  ##  5 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-07-30  ##  6 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-10-29  ##  7 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-09-01  ##  8 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-08  ##  9 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-24  ## 10 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Northern Deep…     29.5      35.0 2025-10-27  ## # ℹ 132 more rows daily_sst <- gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%   get_zonal_statistics("Daily Sea Surface Temperature", n_days = 30, radius = 100, spatial_stats = "mean") daily_sst <- daily_sst %>%   select(site, sample_date, hard_coral_cover, zonal_statistics)  daily_sst ## # A tibble: 142 × 4 ##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover zonal_statistics  ##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl> <list>            ##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6 <tibble [30 × 5]> ## # ℹ 132 more rows

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: zonal statistics returned format need expanded. , can see contain daily mean SST values:
code: daily_sst %>%   unnest(zonal_statistics) ## # A tibble: 3,738 × 8 ##    site                sample_date hard_coral_cover covariate                     ##    <chr>               <date>                 <dbl> <chr>                         ##  1 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  2 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  3 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  4 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  5 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  6 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  7 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  8 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##  9 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ## 10 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature ##    date        band spatial_stat value ##    <date>     <dbl> <chr>        <dbl> ##  1 2023-05-24     1 mean          30.6 ##  2 2023-05-23     1 mean          30.5 ##  3 2023-05-22     1 mean          30.5 ##  4 2023-05-21     1 mean          30.5 ##  5 2023-05-20     1 mean          30.5 ##  6 2023-05-19     1 mean          30.4 ##  7 2023-05-18     1 mean          30.1 ##  8 2023-05-17     1 mean          30.2 ##  9 2023-05-16     1 mean          30.0 ## 10 2023-05-15     1 mean          30.0 ## # ℹ 3,728 more rows

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: get maximum SST 30 day period, use summarise_zonal_statistics(), specifying temporal statistic max: original zonal statistics returned along summarised zonal statistics, expand. Now, one value sample event – maximum SST. returned along start end date covariate data used, well number dates. useful information cases covariate data may available requested dates.
code: max_sst <- daily_sst %>%   summarise_zonal_statistics("max")  max_sst ## # A tibble: 142 × 5 ##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover zonal_statistics  ##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl> <list>            ##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46   <tibble [30 × 5]> ##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5 <tibble [30 × 5]> ## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6 <tibble [30 × 5]> ##    summary_zonal_statistics ##    <list>                   ##  1 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  2 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  3 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  4 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  5 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  6 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  7 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  8 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ##  9 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ## 10 <tibble [1 × 8]>         ## # ℹ 132 more rows max_sst <- max_sst %>%   select(-zonal_statistics) %>%   unnest(summary_zonal_statistics)  max_sst ## # A tibble: 142 × 11 ##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover ##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl> ##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5 ##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2 ##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6 ##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15   ##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63   ##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5 ##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5 ##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46   ##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5 ## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6 ##    covariate                     start_date end_date   n_dates  band spatial_stat ##    <chr>                         <date>     <date>       <dbl> <dbl> <chr>        ##  1 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-25 2023-05-24      30     1 mean         ##  2 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-25 2023-05-24      30     1 mean         ##  3 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean         ##  4 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean         ##  5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean         ##  6 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-29 2023-05-28      30     1 mean         ##  7 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-29 2023-05-28      30     1 mean         ##  8 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-30 2023-05-29      30     1 mean         ##  9 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-05-16 2023-06-14      30     1 mean         ## 10 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-05-18 2023-06-16      30     1 mean         ##    temporal_stat value ##    <chr>         <dbl> ##  1 max            30.6 ##  2 max            30.6 ##  3 max            30.6 ##  4 max            30.6 ##  5 max            30.6 ##  6 max            30.7 ##  7 max            30.7 ##  8 max            30.7 ##  9 max            30.2 ## 10 max            30.3 ## # ℹ 132 more rows

title: Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
text: Finally, visualize coral cover maximum Sea Surface Temperature past 30 days.
code: ggplot(   max_sst,   aes(     x = value,     y = hard_coral_cover   ) ) +   geom_point(color = "darkblue", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +   labs(     title = paste("Coral Cover vs. Max. SST (Previous 30 Days)"),     x = "Maximum SST",     y = "Hard Coral Cover (%)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Sharla Gelfand. Author, maintainer. Wildlife Conservation Society. Copyright holder, funder.

text: Gelfand S (2026). mermaidrcovariates: Access covariates MERMAID data. R package version 1.0.0, https://data-mermaid.github.io/mermaidr-covariates/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mermaidrcovariates: Access covariates for MERMAID data},   author = {Sharla Gelfand},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://data-mermaid.github.io/mermaidr-covariates/}, }

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: goal mermaidrcovariates easily access covariates data add MERMAID data. information detailed instructions usage, please visit package website. details using mermaidr general, please see mermaidr package website.

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: can install mermaidrcovariates GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("data-mermaid/mermaidr-covariates")

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: mermaidrcovariates, can easily add covariates aggregated data coral reef surveys, already entered MERMAID. , first load mermaidrcovariates mermaid packages, access sample events given project. example, work sample events available Benthic PIT method. get covariates, need sample event’s date well latitude longitude. keep columns along site name, using tidyverse package data manipulation.
code: library(mermaidrcovariates) library(mermaidr)  se <- mermaid_search_my_projects("Great Sea Reef 2019") %>%   mermaid_get_project_data("benthicpit", data = "sampleevents", limit = 10) library(tidyverse)  se <- se %>%   select(site, sample_date, latitude, longitude)

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: can use Prescient Browser find covariates available. see programatically, use list_covariates() function: Covariates may contain raster data, vector data, . different functions accessing raster vector data. table summarises function use different covariates available Prescient: can also use function covariate_helper() give information data type function use, well specifications might needed.
code: covariates <- list_covariates() covariates #> # A tibble: 17 × 11 #>    title  description start_date end_date   license `sci:doi` keywords providers #>    <chr>  <chr>       <date>     <date>     <chr>   <chr>     <chr>    <list>    #>  1 50 Re… This datas… 2026-02-22 2026-02-22 CC-BY-… 10.5281/… conserv… <tibble>  #>  2 ACA B… The Alan C… 2018-01-01 2021-01-01 propri… <NA>      ACA, Al… <tibble>  #>  3 ACA R… ACA reef e… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      reef, r… <tibble>  #>  4 Count… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble>  #>  5 Daily… Sea surfac… 1985-01-01 2026-01-13 CC0-1.0 <NA>      climate… <tibble>  #>  6 Human… Coastal po… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coastal… <tibble>  #>  7 Marin… A biogeogr… 2011-11-18 2011-11-18 CC-BY-… 10.1641/… biodive… <tibble>  #>  8 Marke… From econo… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  #>  9 Numbe… Port locat… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  #> 10 Touri… Intensive … 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble>  #> 11 GPW C… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble>  #> 12 GPW D… Dispersal … 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      dispers… <tibble>  #> 13 GPW G… Global Sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 propri… <NA>      exposur… <tibble>  #> 14 GPW G… Global sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, se… <tibble>  #> 15 GPW L… Land Use a… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0-1.0 <NA>      land co… <tibble>  #> 16 GPW M… Marine Eco… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 cc-by-… <NA>      biodive… <tibble>  #> 17 GPW W… Watersheds… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, hy… <tibble>  #> # ℹ 3 more variables: `sci:citation` <chr>, bbox <list>, id <chr>

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: example, look “Daily Sea Surface Temperature” (SST). Running helper function tells us use get_zonal_statistics(), need specify dataset band, since one . want maximum SST site past 30 days, first get daily SST using get_zonal_statistics(), specifying n_days = 30. request data within radius 1000 metres site, function spatially aggregates data 30 days. can see results sample event contains 30 rows – one value 30 days requested. expand zonal statistics, use unnest(), able see mean value date: Since like maximum SST site 30 days, use aggregate_zonal_statistics(), specifying temporal summary statistic max: retain original zonal_statistics column, contains daily values, now also summary_zonal_statistics column, contains one row sample event. expand see summary start end date data used sample event, well spatial temporal statistics used summarise, actual value:
code: covariate_helper("Daily Sea Surface Temperature") #> ℹ Covariate contains *raster* data. Use get_zonal_statistics(). #>   There is only one dataset, so you do not need to specify. #>   There is only one band of data, so you do not need to specify. daily_sst <- get_zonal_statistics(se,   covariate = "Daily Sea Surface Temperature",   spatial_stats = "mean", radius = 1000, n_days = 30 )  daily_sst #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    site  sample_date latitude longitude zonal_statistics  #>    <chr> <date>         <dbl>     <dbl> <list>            #>  1 LW04  2019-09-25     -17.6      177. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  2 BA09  2019-09-26     -17.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  3 BA16  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  4 BA15  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  5 BA11  2019-09-27     -17.3      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  6 YA02  2019-09-30     -17.0      177. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  7 YQ02  2019-10-03     -16.6      179. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  8 IP3.5 2019-10-04     -16.4      179. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  9 GS03  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #> 10 GS05  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> daily_sst %>%   select(site, sample_date, zonal_statistics) %>%   unnest(zonal_statistics) #> # A tibble: 300 × 7 #>    site  sample_date covariate                     date        band spatial_stat #>    <chr> <date>      <chr>                         <date>     <dbl> <chr>        #>  1 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-25     1 mean         #>  2 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-24     1 mean         #>  3 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-23     1 mean         #>  4 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-22     1 mean         #>  5 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-21     1 mean         #>  6 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-20     1 mean         #>  7 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-19     1 mean         #>  8 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-18     1 mean         #>  9 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-17     1 mean         #> 10 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-16     1 mean         #>    value #>    <dbl> #>  1  26.5 #>  2  26.5 #>  3  26.5 #>  4  26.7 #>  5  27.0 #>  6  26.9 #>  7  26.8 #>  8  26.7 #>  9  26.7 #> 10  26.8 #> # ℹ 290 more rows max_sst <- daily_sst %>%   summarise_zonal_statistics("max")  max_sst #> # A tibble: 10 × 6 #>    site  sample_date latitude longitude zonal_statistics  #>    <chr> <date>         <dbl>     <dbl> <list>            #>  1 LW04  2019-09-25     -17.6      177. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  2 BA09  2019-09-26     -17.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  3 BA16  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  4 BA15  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  5 BA11  2019-09-27     -17.3      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  6 YA02  2019-09-30     -17.0      177. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  7 YQ02  2019-10-03     -16.6      179. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  8 IP3.5 2019-10-04     -16.4      179. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>  9 GS03  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #> 10 GS05  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. <tibble [30 × 5]> #>    summary_zonal_statistics #>    <list>                   #>  1 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  2 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  3 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  4 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  5 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  6 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  7 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  8 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #>  9 <tibble [1 × 8]>         #> 10 <tibble [1 × 8]> max_sst %>%   select(site, sample_date, summary_zonal_statistics) %>%   unnest(summary_zonal_statistics) #> # A tibble: 10 × 10 #>    site  sample_date covariate                     start_date end_date   n_dates #>    <chr> <date>      <chr>                         <date>     <date>       <dbl> #>  1 LW04  2019-09-25  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-08-27 2019-09-25      30 #>  2 BA09  2019-09-26  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-08-28 2019-09-26      30 #>  3 BA16  2019-09-27  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-08-29 2019-09-27      30 #>  4 BA15  2019-09-27  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-08-29 2019-09-27      30 #>  5 BA11  2019-09-27  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-08-29 2019-09-27      30 #>  6 YA02  2019-09-30  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-01 2019-09-30      30 #>  7 YQ02  2019-10-03  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-04 2019-10-03      30 #>  8 IP3.5 2019-10-04  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-05 2019-10-04      30 #>  9 GS03  2019-10-08  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-09 2019-10-08      30 #> 10 GS05  2019-10-08  Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2019-09-09 2019-10-08      30 #>     band spatial_stat temporal_stat value #>    <dbl> <chr>        <chr>         <dbl> #>  1     1 mean         max            27.2 #>  2     1 mean         max            27.0 #>  3     1 mean         max            27.0 #>  4     1 mean         max            27.0 #>  5     1 mean         max            27   #>  6     1 mean         max            27.0 #>  7     1 mean         max            27.4 #>  8     1 mean         max            27.7 #>  9     1 mean         max            27.5 #> 10     1 mean         max            27.4

title: Access covariates for MERMAID data
text: want attach vector data, Country Boundaries, use attach_vector_data(). , covariate_helper() function useful determining : tells us function use, need specify dataset covariate. also tells us , default, columns vector data returned, can limit specific columns. attach covariate data: case, format needs expanded – columns immediately available. want one column (e.g. country name), can specify function:
code: covariate_helper("Country Boundaries") #> ℹ Covariate contains *vector* data. Use attach_covariates(). #>   There is only one dataset, so you do not need to specify. #>   By default, all columns will be returned. You can limit to specific colunmns in `columns` argument. #>   Column options: "COUNTRY_ID", "TERRITORY1", "UN_TER1", "geom" se_country_all_cols <- se %>%   attach_covariate_data("Country Boundaries")  se_country_all_cols #> # A tibble: 10 × 7 #>    site  sample_date latitude longitude COUNTRY_ID TERRITORY1 UN_TER1 #>    <chr> <date>         <dbl>     <dbl>      <int> <chr>        <dbl> #>  1 BA09  2019-09-26     -17.4      178.         54 Fiji           242 #>  2 BA16  2019-09-27     -17.2      178.         54 Fiji           242 #>  3 GS03  2019-10-08     -16.4      178.         54 Fiji           242 #>  4 BA15  2019-09-27     -17.2      178.         54 Fiji           242 #>  5 YA02  2019-09-30     -17.0      177.         54 Fiji           242 #>  6 LW04  2019-09-25     -17.6      177.         54 Fiji           242 #>  7 IP3.5 2019-10-04     -16.4      179.         54 Fiji           242 #>  8 BA11  2019-09-27     -17.3      178.         54 Fiji           242 #>  9 GS05  2019-10-08     -16.4      178.         54 Fiji           242 #> 10 YQ02  2019-10-03     -16.6      179.         54 Fiji           242 se_country <- se %>%   attach_covariate_data("Country Boundaries", columns = "TERRITORY1")  se_country #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>    site  sample_date latitude longitude TERRITORY1 #>    <chr> <date>         <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>      #>  1 BA09  2019-09-26     -17.4      178. Fiji       #>  2 BA16  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. Fiji       #>  3 GS03  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. Fiji       #>  4 BA15  2019-09-27     -17.2      178. Fiji       #>  5 YA02  2019-09-30     -17.0      177. Fiji       #>  6 LW04  2019-09-25     -17.6      177. Fiji       #>  7 IP3.5 2019-10-04     -16.4      179. Fiji       #>  8 BA11  2019-09-27     -17.3      178. Fiji       #>  9 GS05  2019-10-08     -16.4      178. Fiji       #> 10 YQ02  2019-10-03     -16.6      179. Fiji

title: Attach covariate data — attach_covariate_data
text: Attach covariate data vector format. date dependent – attach based sample event location.

title: Attach covariate data — attach_covariate_data
code: attach_covariate_data(   se,   covariate,   dataset = NULL,   columns = NULL,   date_col = "sample_date" )

title: Attach covariate data — attach_covariate_data
text: se Sample events mermaidr covariate Covariate attach. covariate title ID permitted. Run list_covariates() see available covariates. dataset Dataset within covariate. required one dataset. Run list_datasets_for_covariate see datasets columns. columns Columns within dataset. NULL (default), returns columns. Run list_datasets_for_covariate see datasets bands. date_col Date column data. Defaults "sample_date".

title: Covariate helper — covariate_helper
text: Tells function use get covariates data! Also returns information datasets, bands columns, .

title: Covariate helper — covariate_helper
code: covariate_helper(covariate)

title: Covariate helper — covariate_helper
text: covariate Covariate name ID

title: Covariate helper — covariate_helper
code: # covariate_helper("Daily Sea Surface Temperature") # covariate_helper("GPW MEOW Realms")

title: Get zonal statistics — get_zonal_statistics
text: Get zonal statistics covariate data raster format.. Using spatial_stats spatially summarise covariates data, relevant date prior sample event date. example, daily covariate like Daily Sea Surface Temperature, function returns SST ** n_days prior sample event date. covariate periodic, e.g. occurring every 5 years, returns recent value. covariate occurs (e.g. 50 Reefs+ prioritization), returns data.

title: Get zonal statistics — get_zonal_statistics
code: get_zonal_statistics(   se,   covariate,   spatial_stats = "mean",   radius = 100,   n_days = NULL,   dataset = NULL,   bands = NULL,   date_col = "sample_date",   .progress = TRUE )

title: Get zonal statistics — get_zonal_statistics
text: se Sample events mermaidr covariate Covariate get statistics . covariate title ID permitted. Run list_covariates see available covariates. spatial_stats Spatial statistics – used summarise data around site location, according radius set. radius 0, spatial_stats relevant; just value . n_days Number days get statistics . Includes sample date , days prior – e.g., 365 days include sample date 364 days prior. relevant covariates daily; otherwise ignored. dataset Dataset within covariate. required cases, one dataset. Run list_datasets_for_covariate see datasets bands. bands Bands within dataset. required cases, one band. required, can numeric named band. Run list_datasets_for_covariate see datasets bands. date_col Sample date column – used date-dependent covariates (e.g. daily periodic). Defaults "sample_date". .progress Whether show progress bar time remaining. Defaults TRUE.

title: List available covariates — list_covariates
text: List available covariates

title: List available covariates — list_covariates
code: list_covariates(as_data_frame = TRUE)

title: List available covariates — list_covariates
text: as_data_frame Whether return covariates data frame. Defaults TRUE.

title: List available covariates — list_covariates
code: # list_covariates()

what: List datasets and bands or columns for a covariate — list_datasets_for_covariate
title: List datasets and bands or columns for a covariate — list_datasets_for_covariate
text: List datasets bands columns covariate

title: List datasets and bands or columns for a covariate — list_datasets_for_covariate
code: list_datasets_for_covariate(covariate)

title: List datasets and bands or columns for a covariate — list_datasets_for_covariate
text: covariate Covariate

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: See magrittr::%>% details.

title: Pipe operator — %>%
code: lhs %>% rhs

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: lhs value magrittr placeholder. rhs function call using magrittr semantics.

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: result calling `rhs(lhs)`.

title: Search available covariates — search_covariates
text: Search available covariates

title: Search available covariates — search_covariates
code: search_covariates(title, description, as_data_frame = TRUE)

title: Search available covariates — search_covariates
text: title Search term within title. Case insensitive. description Search term within description. Case insensitive. as_data_frame Whether return covariates data frame. Defaults TRUE.

title: Search available covariates — search_covariates
code: # search_covariates("MEOW")

title: Summarise zonal statistics — summarise_zonal_statistics
text: Summarise zonal statistics. Using results  get_zonal_statistics, summarises data *`n_days`* prior sample event date, using `temporal_stats` summarise resulting data time.

title: Summarise zonal statistics — summarise_zonal_statistics
code: summarise_zonal_statistics(   zonal_statistics,   temporal_stats = c("min", "max", "mean") )

title: Summarise zonal statistics — summarise_zonal_statistics
text: zonal_statistics Sample events zonal statistics, get_zonal_statistics. temporal_stats Temporal statistics – used summarise data time.

title: mermaidrcovariates 1.0.0
text: Added attach_covariate_data() accessing vector covariates Added covariate_helper() understanding attach_covariate_data() get_zonal_statistics() use Added list_datasets_for_covariate() listing datasets bands/columns within covariate Improved parallelization Large rewrite zonal statistics API: Deprecated get_summary_zonal_statistics() favour using get_zonal_statistics() thensummarise_zonal_statistics() Renamed covariates column get_zonal_statistics() zonal_statistics Renamed covariates column summarise_zonal_statistics() summary_zonal_statistics – original zonal_statistics() column retained Removed columns start_date, end_date, n_dates get_zonal_statistics() output – appear output summarise_zonal_statistics() Added support multiple bands, specifying dataset

title: mermaidrcovariates 0.1.2
text: Add search_covariates() search title description Add date_col get_zonal_statistics() get_summary_zonal_statistics(), allows using column "sample_date".

title: mermaidrcovariates 0.1.1
text: Fix bug calculating n_dates returned get_summary_zonal_statistics(). Fix bug get_zonal_statistics() get_summary_zonal_statistics() stripping columns se returned data.

title: mermaidrcovariates 0.1.0
text: list_covariates() returns data frame default, print method list (as_data_frame = FALSE) improved. get_zonal_statistics() takes covariate name ID (previously just ID). argument name covariate instead covariate_id. get_zonal_statistics() returns n_dates covariates column, gets stats n_days - 1 prior sample date sample date. get_zonal_statistics() now returns zonal statistics n_days, get_summary_zonal_statistics() summarises zonal statistics time. get_summary_zonal_statistics() requires spatial_stats – summarise spatially – temporal_stats – summarise time. returns columns covariates, along value. get_zonal_statistics() requires spatial_stats. returns column covariates, along value. argument buffer renamed radius.

title: mermaidrcovariates 0.0.1
text: Initial MVP.
